name: Norse Mythology
title: Eir - Norse Mythology
description: Also known as 💉 Eir. Associated with healing norse mythology, 💉 eir, healing.

keyMyths.title: Eir's Healing Touch
keyMyths.description: Eir is called upon by the gods to heal their wounds and ailments, showcasing her unparalleled skill in medicine. She uses herbs and ancient remedies to restore balance and vitality to those she tends to.

keyMyths.title: Attending Menglöð
keyMyths.description: Eir is among the maidens who serve Menglöð on Lyfjaberg (Healing Hill), indicating her role as a leader or prominent figure in the realm of healing. She assists Menglöð in providing aid to those who seek it.

keyMyths.title: The Mystery of Her Origins
keyMyths.description: Eir's origins remain shrouded in mystery, with some scholars suggesting she may be an aspect of Frigg or another goddess associated with healing. Her true parentage is never explicitly stated.

keyMyths.title: Eir's Knowledge of Herbs
keyMyths.description: Eir possesses extensive knowledge of herbs and their medicinal properties, using this knowledge to create potent remedies. Her expertise is sought after by gods and mortals alike.

keyMyths.title: Eir and the Valkyries
keyMyths.description: While not explicitly a Valkyrie, some interpretations suggest Eir's association with healing battlescarred warriors links her to the Valkyries. This connection highlights the importance of healing in the context of warfare and heroism.
keyMyths.source: Hypothetical reconstructions based on her role
